title: To Marinianus, Bishop of Ravenna.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: John, the bearer of these presents, complains that his wife, flying from the molestations of one George, has long been residing within venerable precincts, and has so far met with no assistance.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Since she asserts that there is a dispute about her condition, and has asked that it should be commended to your Fraternity, we hereby exhort you that you afford your protection to this woman, and permit her not to be in any way aggrieved by any one unreasonably.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But if the question about her station still continues, let it be your care that, without any oppression, and in a legal manner, it may be submitted for judgment; so that when, after ascertainment of the truth, what is agreeable to the order of law has been determined, neither party may complain of having suffered wrong.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: The month of May, first Indiction.
